name: Get Index List
summary: Retrieves the list of indices for a given exchange. A POST call returns an array of "Basket, Criteria pair" objects, each containing the index name (idxname) and the token used to subscribe to it.

request_params.name: jData
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list

response_fields.name: stat
response_fields.description: Limits request success or failure indication.

response_fields.name: values
response_fields.description: Array of Basket, Criteria pair objects (each with idxname and token).

response_fields.name: request_time
response_fields.description: This will be present only in a successful response.

response_fields.name: emsg
response_fields.description: This will be present only in case of errors.

response_fields.name: idxname
response_fields.description: Basket, Criteria pair object field: Index Name

response_fields.name: token
response_fields.description: Basket, Criteria pair object field: Index token used to subscribe

notes: The "values" response field is an array of "Basket, Criteria pair" objects; each object has two fields: idxname (Index Name) and token (Index token used to subscribe). The curl example in the source uses a placeholder host 'https://BaseURL/GetIndexList', but the documented real endpoint is https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI/GetIndexList. Request payload is sent as form-style data: jData={json}&jKey={token}. A successful response includes request_time (format "HH:MM:SS DD-MM-YYYY", e.g. "20:12:29 13-12-2020"); emsg appears only on errors. The sample success JSON in the source (titled "Sample Output:") shows only the values array and request_time (no top-level stat field) and ends with a trailing comma after the last array element — preserved verbatim.
issues: No standalone sample failure response is shown for this endpoint (only the general note that emsg appears on error and stat may be Not_Ok). The curl example uses a placeholder 'BaseURL' rather than the literal host. The sample success JSON contains a trailing comma before the closing bracket (preserved verbatim) and omits the top-level stat field. No "Possible value" cells are populated for jData/jKey/uid/exch/request_time/emsg/idxname/token in the source tables (those cells are blank); only stat ("Ok or Not_Ok") and values ("Array Of Basket, Criteria pair.") have possible-value entries.
